assumption_failure: Assumes non-expert operators can interpret gate failures and safely use waiver tokens
abuse_or_break_mode: Users normalize failures or reuse long-lived waivers that convert fail-closed into fail-open
required_hardening_patch: Enforce short-lived signed waivers with reason codes approver separation max-use counts and CI rejection of wildcard or expired waivers

assumption_failure: Assumes layered detectors are tuned and maintainable without false-positive fatigue
abuse_or_break_mode: Teams over-allow noisy findings and attackers evade single detectors with format shifts
required_hardening_patch: Ship baseline tuning profiles mandatory allowlist expiry and review detector ensemble voting and regression corpus for obfuscated secrets

assumption_failure: Assumes required-subset schema compatibility will be implemented consistently
abuse_or_break_mode: Schema drift or parser ambiguity allows malformed logs to pass or blocks legitimate logs
required_hardening_patch: Publish canonical schema contract tests pin schema compatibility matrix reject unknown critical columns and require migration linter in CI

assumption_failure: Assumes freshness metadata and artifact lineage are trustworthy
abuse_or_break_mode: Stale or fabricated artifacts with forged timestamps pass checks
required_hardening_patch: Require signed artifact manifests with monotonic versioning trusted time-source validation issuer allowlist and scoped exception templates

assumption_failure: Assumes signature or checksum trust anchors are well-managed
abuse_or_break_mode: Compromised keys or weak bootstrap lets poisoned feed pass compliance checks
required_hardening_patch: Implement key rotation policy threshold signatures pinned offline root immutable snapshot verification and last-known-good rollback

assumption_failure: Assumes executable misuse assertions represent real ML abuse paths
abuse_or_break_mode: Assertions become shallow and teams pass tests without meaningful coverage
required_hardening_patch: Bind assertions to threat scenario IDs require mutation-test quality gates and fail CI on missing coverage deltas

assumption_failure: Assumes governance evidence artifacts are authentic complete and commit-bound
abuse_or_break_mode: Stale or copied artifacts satisfy presence checks and produce false assurance
required_hardening_patch: Cryptographically attest test artifacts bind evidence to commit SHA and run ID enforce freshness windows and independent verifier step

assumption_failure: Assumes adversarial numeric thresholds remain meaningful over time
abuse_or_break_mode: Thresholds are lowered to unblock releases or overfit known test sets
required_hardening_patch: Protect threshold changes with approval policy maintain holdout benchmark sets and auto-fail on statistically significant degradation

assumption_failure: Assumes sanitizer and policy gates cover every instruction channel
abuse_or_break_mode: Indirect prompt injection through retrieval or tool output bypasses boundary checks
required_hardening_patch: Apply taint tracking across context sources default-deny tool policy for untrusted context and mandatory signed intent for privileged actions

assumption_failure: Assumes least-authority scopes and signed intent are mapped correctly
abuse_or_break_mode: Scope gaps or reusable tokens allow cross-repo destructive actions through unintended paths
required_hardening_patch: Enforce per-action capability tokens nonce-bound one-time signatures repo and path allowlists and centralized deny-by-default authorization audit

assumption_failure: Assumes high-risk diff patterns are sufficient proxies for unsafe AI logic
abuse_or_break_mode: Novel insecure logic bypasses pattern blockers and gets merged
required_hardening_patch: Add semantic code policy checks with dataflow and sink rules require test evidence for sensitive edits and mandatory human review on high impact files

assumption_failure: Assumes semantic DLP quarantine will be reviewed promptly
abuse_or_break_mode: Alert backlog creates auto-approval pressure and transformed data slips through
required_hardening_patch: Set quarantine SLAs confidence-tiered escalation dual review requirements and fail-closed behavior for unresolved high-risk findings

assumption_failure: Assumes offline exceptions remain rare and bounded
abuse_or_break_mode: Repeated renewals become permanent stale-data bypass
required_hardening_patch: Implement hard exception budget renewal cooldown mandatory justification taxonomy and automatic stop on cumulative offline SLA breach

assumption_failure: Assumes deterministic sampling plus floor captures important CVE risk
abuse_or_break_mode: High-risk issues outside sample remain underweighted while reports still pass
required_hardening_patch: Use risk-stratified sampling with mandatory inclusion rules and omitted-set risk summaries and block publish on under-covered critical strata

assumption_failure: Assumes threshold ordering cannot be bypassed and inputs stay fresh
abuse_or_break_mode: Stale or conflicting inputs cause inconsistent remediation outcomes
required_hardening_patch: Encode policy as deterministic decision graph with signed freshness checks precedence conflict tests and operator-safe explanations in immutable audit trail

assumption_failure: Assumes stale-intel detection and privileged-path classification are complete
abuse_or_break_mode: Users route actions through equivalent non-privileged paths to bypass stale blocks
required_hardening_patch: Classify actions by effective impact and apply stale-data blocks to all equivalent-impact paths with tamper-evident stale-state attestations
